cotton-conventional.name: Conventional Cotton
cotton-conventional.description: Grown with synthetic pesticides and fertilisers; very thirsty (≈10,000 L/kg). Most common apparel fibre worldwide.

cotton-organic.name: Organic Cotton
cotton-organic.description: No synthetic pesticides; lower water and energy than conventional cotton; look for GOTS.

recycled-cotton.name: Recycled Cotton
recycled-cotton.description: Mechanically or chemically recycled from textile waste; saves water and avoids virgin farming.

polyester.name: Polyester
polyester.description: Petroleum-based; energy-intensive; sheds microplastics in every wash.

recycled-polyester.name: Recycled Polyester (rPET)
recycled-polyester.description: Made from post-consumer bottles or textile waste; much lower footprint than virgin polyester.

nylon.name: Nylon (Polyamide)
nylon.description: High energy use; nitrous oxide from production; common in activewear and hosiery.

recycled-nylon.name: Recycled Nylon
recycled-nylon.description: Often from fishing nets or industrial scrap; strong performance with lower impact.

elastane.name: Elastane / Spandex / Lycra®
elastane.description: Almost always blended in small %; fossil-based and hard to recycle at end of life.

acrylic.name: Acrylic
acrylic.description: Wool-like synthetic; energy-intensive; can release microfibres.

wool.name: Wool (Virgin)
wool.description: Durable and biodegradable; methane from sheep is a major part of footprint.

organic-wool.name: Organic Wool
organic-wool.description: Pasture and animal welfare standards; fewer synthetic inputs than conventional wool.

recycled-wool.name: Recycled Wool
recycled-wool.description: Reclaimed from pre- or post-consumer knitwear; excellent low-impact option.

merino-wool.name: Merino Wool
merino-wool.description: Fine fibre for base layers; similar land and methane considerations as wool.

cashmere.name: Cashmere
cashmere.description: Very high land use and degradation per kg; luxury staple with outsized impact.

recycled-cashmere.name: Recycled Cashmere
recycled-cashmere.description: Blends of reclaimed cashmere fibres; far better than virgin cashmere.

alpaca.name: Alpaca Fibre
alpaca.description: Soft luxury fibre; lower methane per animal than sheep but still animal agriculture.

mohair.name: Mohair
mohair.description: From angora goats; durable sheen; welfare and land use vary by farm.

angora.name: Angora (Rabbit)
angora.description: Very fine; ethical sourcing critical — many brands have phased out.

silk.name: Silk
silk.description: Boiling cocoons uses energy and water; biodegradable and long-lasting.

down-feather.name: Down & Feather
down-feather.description: Fill for jackets and bedding; traceability and live-plucking concerns matter.

linen.name: Linen (Flax)
linen.description: Flax needs little irrigation and few pesticides; strong and biodegradable.

hemp.name: Hemp
hemp.description: Fast-growing, low inputs; excellent for denim, tees, and blends.

ramie.name: Ramie
ramie.description: Bast fibre like linen; lustrous; processing can be chemical-heavy.

jute.name: Jute
jute.description: Coarse bast fibre; bags and linings; very low impact crop.

tencel.name: TENCEL™ / Lyocell
tencel.description: Wood pulp in closed-loop solvent recovery; soft drape, biodegradable.

modal.name: Modal
modal.description: Regenerated cellulose like viscose; often from beech; check responsible forestry.

viscose.name: Viscose / Rayon
viscose.description: Wood pulp with chemical spinning; can drive deforestation if not certified.

cupro.name: Cupro / Bemberg™
cupro.description: Regenerated from cotton linter; silk-like hand; chemical recovery matters.

acetate.name: Acetate
acetate.description: Lining fabric from wood pulp; less common in casual wear today.

bamboo-viscose.name: Bamboo Viscose
bamboo-viscose.description: Often marketed as eco; chemically identical to rayon unless lyocell process.

bamboo.name: Bamboo (Generic)
bamboo.description: Fast-growing grass; impact depends heavily on processing (viscose vs mechanical).

leather.name: Leather (Chrome Tan)
leather.description: Livestock + tanning; very high water and chemical load; long-lived products help amortise.

vegetable-tanned-leather.name: Vegetable-Tanned Leather
vegetable-tanned-leather.description: Traditional tannins; avoids chrome but still livestock-intensive.

vegan-leather.name: Vegan Leather (PU / PVC)
vegan-leather.description: Plastic-based; lower than leather on some metrics but fossil-derived and hard to recycle.

pinatex.name: Piñatex® (Pineapple Leaf)
pinatex.description: Uses agricultural waste from pineapples; bio-based coating options exist.

cork.name: Cork Fabric
cork.description: Harvested from bark without killing trees; carbon-negative cork oak landscapes.

rubber-natural.name: Natural Rubber
rubber-natural.description: Soles and elastic; from latex trees; deforestation risk if not responsibly sourced.

recycled-rubber.name: Recycled Rubber
recycled-rubber.description: Tyre and post-industrial scrap for soles and mats.

neoprene.name: Neoprene
neoprene.description: Wetsuits and structured garments; synthetic rubber, high embodied energy.

pvc.name: PVC / Vinyl
pvc.description: Coatings, shiny fashion, rainwear; chlorine chemistry and plasticisers are concerns.

polyurethane-coating.name: Polyurethane (PU) Coating
polyurethane-coating.description: Waterproof layers on jackets; thinner than full PVC in many cases.

fleece-polyester.name: Polar Fleece (Polyester)
fleece-polyester.description: Warm mid-layers; high microfibre shed — wash in a bag and prefer recycled content.

sequin-metallic.name: Sequins / Metallic Films
sequin-metallic.description: PET or metalised plastic on mesh; hard to recycle and sheds particles.
